_note: Sidecar for the cruise-gear buyer's-guide article. Affiliate-monetized (gear only). All amzn.to links are existing, operator-provided links pulled from .claude/plan-affiliate-deployment.md — none invented. NO affiliate links on health/medication items per the binding bright line in admin/W4-FTC-AFFILIATE-POSTURE.md.

affiliate_posture.tag_mechanism: Bare amzn.to short links (associate tag embedded in the shortlink), matching the existing implementation in articles/cruise-cabin-organization.html. rel="sponsored noopener" on every affiliate link; FTC disclosure block near top matching the house pattern.
affiliate_posture.links_source: .claude/plan-affiliate-deployment.md (Sections 2 and 4). Every code above is copied verbatim from that inventory; none fabricated.

health_products_excluded.rule: admin/W4-FTC-AFFILIATE-POSTURE.md Section 2.1 + Section 4 decision tree: permanent bright line, no medication/health-product affiliate links (motion-sickness patches, Sea-Bands, anti-nausea wristbands explicitly named).
health_products_excluded.conflict_resolved: The DRAFT deployment plan (.claude/plan-affiliate-deployment.md, 2026-01-18, Status: Draft) Section 4 still lists a 'Motion Sickness Relief' affiliate table (Dramamine amzn.to/4qCaZ2L, Bonine 45N4G3W, patches 49Zdd6q, Sea-Band 4sQHlsc, SafeHarbor 3NqBwl6, Reliefband 4jJm7s9). This is STALE: the newer FTC posture doc (2026-05-06, v2.5) supersedes it and states the Sea-Bands link was removed under Phase A. Per CLAUDE.md conflict order (pastoral/integrity over convenience), the binding bright line wins. Those six links were deliberately NOT used. The seasickness section in this article names the remedies generically and links only to the free seasickness guide.
health_products_excluded.operator_instruction: User said 'include seasick stuff' — honored by including a seasickness section, named but unlinked, with an explicit on-page statement that health products carry no affiliate links.

power_strip_ban.claim: Royal Caribbean and Celebrity ban surge protectors / most AC power strips; other lines restrict them; USB-only blocks are the safe universal choice
power_strip_ban.source: Cruise-line policy, asserted in .claude/plan-affiliate-deployment.md Section 4 (Power Strips note: 'Royal Caribbean/Celebrity now ban ALL AC-outlet devices'). Framed in-article with a 'check your line's current policy' hedge rather than as a permanent absolute.
power_strip_ban.disposition: stated with hedge; not a hard dated claim

power_bank_carry_on.claim: Power banks must travel in carry-on, never checked luggage
power_bank_carry_on.source: Airline lithium-battery safety rule (TSA), noted in the deployment plan Section 4 ('MUST be in carry-on, never checked luggage'). Well-established.

seasickness_remedies.claim: meclizine (Bonine, less drowsy), dimenhydrinate (Dramamine), scopolamine patch (Rx, longer/rougher); wristbands and ginger have weak evidence
seasickness_remedies.source: CDC Yellow Book — Motion Sickness (carried from cruise-seasickness-what-works.factcheck.json, verified 2026-06-18). Framed as 'ask your doctor'; no efficacy overclaim.

voice_audit.authorship_mode: Affiliate buyer's guide. House pattern followed: every product carries an honest 'skip it if…' counter-note; no banned cruise-marketing vocab; plain copulas; recommendations framed as problem-solving, not selling. Pastoral note from the deployment plan honored (range of price points, nothing pushed at vulnerable/budget readers).
voice_audit.risk_rating_justification: Affiliate links restricted to gear; health bright line respected and made explicit on-page; honest counter-notes throughout; distinct angle from cabin-organization (buyer's-guide framing, links out for cabin depth) to avoid keyword cannibalization. Re-confirm against shipped HTML before commit.
voice_audit.outstanding_drift_notes: If cruise-line power-strip policies change, re-verify the RCL/Celebrity claim.
